title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 5: Logic and Reasoning
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Maria bought 3 notebooks at 4 dollars each and 2 pens at 3 dollars each. How many dollars did she spend in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 80' width='220' height='80'><rect x='10' y='20' width='30' height='40' fill='#fdd' stroke='#222'/><rect x='45' y='20' width='30' height='40' fill='#fdd' stroke='#222'/><rect x='80' y='20' width='30' height='40' fill='#fdd' stroke='#222'/><rect x='130' y='30' width='6' height='30' fill='#246' stroke='#222'/><rect x='155' y='30' width='6' height='30' fill='#246' stroke='#222'/><text x='55' y='75' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>notebooks</text><text x='148' y='75' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>pens</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three notebooks and two pens shown side by side.
questions.solution: Notebooks: 3 x 4 = 12 dollars. Pens: 2 x 3 = 6 dollars. Total: 12 + 6 = 18 dollars.

questions.question_text: A rectangle on grid paper measures 5 units wide and 3 units tall. What is its perimeter in units?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 120' width='200' height='120'><rect x='20' y='20' width='150' height='90' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><text x='90' y='15' font-size='12' text-anchor='middle'>5</text><text x='180' y='70' font-size='12'>3</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled with width 5 and height 3.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2(5 + 3) = 2(8) = 16 units.

questions.question_text: In the sequence 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ..., what is the 8th term?
questions.solution: The sequence increases by 3 each step. 8th term = 2 + 3(7) = 2 + 21 = 23.

questions.question_text: Which of the following numbers is divisible by both 3 and 4?
questions.solution: A number divisible by both 3 and 4 must be divisible by 12. Only 24 is a multiple of 12 among the choices.

questions.question_text: Anna is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Cora. Cora is shorter than Dan. Who is definitely the shortest of these four?
questions.solution: Anna > Ben > Cora and Dan > Cora. So Cora is shorter than Ben, Anna, and Dan, making Cora definitely the shortest of the four.

questions.question_text: A movie started at 2:45 PM and ended at 4:20 PM. How many minutes long was the movie?
questions.solution: From 2:45 to 3:45 is 60 minutes. From 3:45 to 4:20 is 35 minutes. Total = 60 + 35 = 95 minutes.

questions.question_text: A square is cut along one diagonal. What shapes are formed?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' width='120' height='120'><rect x='15' y='15' width='90' height='90' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='15' x2='105' y2='105' stroke='#c33' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with one diagonal drawn from corner to corner.
questions.solution: Cutting a square along a diagonal produces two congruent right triangles with a 90-degree angle at the original square corner.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 100 - 37 - 23?
Leo has 3 shirts (red, blue, green) and 2 pairs of pants (black, gray). How many different shirt-and-pants outfits can he make?
questions.solution: Each shirt can be paired with each of 2 pants: 3 x 2 = 6 outfits.

questions.question_text: Which shape comes next in the pattern: circle, square, triangle, circle, square, triangle, circle, ...?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 280 40' width='280' height='40'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='12' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='48' y='8' width='24' height='24' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='100,32 112,8 124,32' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='152' cy='20' r='12' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='180' y='8' width='24' height='24' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='232,32 244,8 256,32' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of circle, square, triangle shown twice.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 shapes: circle, square, triangle. After the 7th shape (circle), the next is square.

questions.question_text: If 3x + 7 = 22, what is the value of x?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' width='200' height='60'><text x='100' y='38' font-size='22' text-anchor='middle' font-family='serif'>3x + 7 = 22</text></svg>

questions.question_text: A figure is made of 5 identical small squares arranged in a plus shape. If each small square has side length 2 cm, what is the total area of the figure in square cm?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 150 150' width='150' height='150'><rect x='55' y='15' width='40' height='40' fill='#cfe' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='15' y='55' width='40' height='40' fill='#cfe' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='55' y='55' width='40' height='40' fill='#cfe' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='95' y='55' width='40' height='40' fill='#cfe' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='55' y='95' width='40' height='40' fill='#cfe' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five squares arranged as a plus sign with one center square and four arms.
questions.solution: Each small square has area 2 x 2 = 4 sq cm. Five squares: 5 x 4 = 20 sq cm.

questions.question_text: Four friends sit in a row. Paul is not at either end. Rita sits to the right of Paul. Sam sits at the left end. Tina sits to the right of Rita. What is the order from left to right?
questions.solution: Sam is at the left end. Paul is not at either end, so Paul is in position 2 or 3. Rita is to the right of Paul, and Tina is to the right of Rita. So the order is Sam, Paul, Rita, Tina.

questions.question_text: In a class of 30 students, the ratio of boys to girls is 2:3. How many girls are in the class?
questions.solution: Total parts = 2 + 3 = 5. Each part = 30 / 5 = 6 students. Girls = 3 x 6 = 18.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all positive divisors of 18?
A water tank holds 240 liters when full. It is currently 3/4 full. How many more liters of water are needed to fill it completely?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 160' width='120' height='160'><rect x='30' y='20' width='60' height='120' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='60' height='90' fill='#8cf' stroke='#222' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A vertical tank 3/4 filled with water shown in blue.
questions.solution: Empty portion is 1/4 of 240 = 60 liters needed.

questions.question_text: How many three-digit numbers can be formed using the digits 1, 2, 3 if each digit is used exactly once?
A train travels at a constant speed of 60 km/h. How many kilometers does it travel in 2 hours and 30 minutes?
questions.solution: 2 hours 30 min = 2.5 hours. Distance = 60 x 2.5 = 150 km.

questions.question_text: The first four triangular numbers are 1, 3, 6, 10. What is the 7th triangular number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 80' width='180' height='80'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='60' cy='20' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='54' cy='40' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='66' cy='40' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='100' cy='20' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='94' cy='40' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='106' cy='40' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='88' cy='60' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='100' cy='60' r='6' fill='#39c'/><circle cx='112' cy='60' r='6' fill='#39c'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Dot arrangements illustrating the first three triangular numbers 1, 3, 6.
questions.solution: Triangular number T_n = n(n+1)/2. T_7 = 7 x 8 / 2 = 28.

questions.question_text: In a row of 5 houses numbered 1 to 5, the red house is next to the blue house. The blue house is between the red and green houses. The yellow house is at position 1. The white house is at position 5. If the green house is at position 4, what is the color of house 3?
questions.solution: Yellow at 1, White at 5, Green at 4. Blue is between red and green, so blue is at 3 and red at 2. House 3 is blue.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive even numbers is 72. What is the largest of these numbers?
questions.solution: Let the numbers be n, n+2, n+4. Sum: 3n + 6 = 72, so n = 22. Largest = 22 + 4 = 26.

questions.question_text: A rectangle is 12 cm long and 8 cm wide. A smaller rectangle of dimensions 4 cm by 3 cm is cut out from one corner. What is the area of the remaining shape in square cm?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 140' width='200' height='140'><polygon points='20,20 180,20 180,100 100,100 100,120 20,120' fill='#ffe' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shaped region formed by removing a small rectangle from the corner of a larger rectangle.
questions.solution: Original area = 12 x 8 = 96. Removed = 4 x 3 = 12. Remaining = 96 - 12 = 84 sq cm.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest positive integer that leaves a remainder of 2 when divided by 3, by 4, and by 5?
questions.solution: Such a number minus 2 must be divisible by lcm(3,4,5) = 60. Smallest positive: 60 + 2 = 62.

questions.question_text: A box contains only red, blue, and yellow balls. There are twice as many blue balls as red balls, and three times as many yellow balls as red balls. If there are 36 balls in total, how many blue balls are there?
questions.solution: Let red = r. Blue = 2r, Yellow = 3r. Total: r + 2r + 3r = 6r = 36, so r = 6. Blue = 2 x 6 = 12.

questions.question_text: How many different paths from A to B are there on a 3 by 2 grid if you can only move right or up along the grid lines?
questions.image_description: A 3 by 2 rectangular grid with A at the bottom-left and B at the top-right.

questions.question_text: A fruit vendor sells apples at 3 for 2 dollars. Emma bought 18 apples and paid with a 20-dollar bill. How many dollars change did she receive?
questions.solution: 18 apples = 18/3 = 6 groups of 3. Cost = 6 x 2 = 12 dollars. Change = 20 - 12 = 8 dollars.

questions.question_text: The perimeter of a rectangle is 30 cm and its length is twice its width. What is the area of the rectangle in square cm?
questions.solution: Let width = w, length = 2w. Perimeter: 2(w + 2w) = 6w = 30, so w = 5. Length = 10. Area = 5 x 10 = 50 sq cm.

questions.question_text: Three brothers tell statements. Alex says: 'Ben is lying.' Ben says: 'Carl is lying.' Carl says: 'Alex and Ben are both lying.' How many of the brothers are telling the truth?
questions.solution: If Alex tells truth, Ben lies, so Carl tells truth, but Carl says both Alex and Ben lie (contradiction since Alex tells truth). So Alex lies. Then Ben tells truth, so Carl lies. Carl says both Alex and Ben lie, which is false since Ben tells truth - consistent (Carl is lying). So exactly 1 brother (Ben) tells the truth.

questions.question_text: Two runners start at the same point and run in opposite directions around a 400-meter track. One runs at 5 m/s and the other at 3 m/s. How many seconds until they first meet?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 120' width='200' height='120'><ellipse cx='100' cy='60' rx='80' ry='40' fill='none' stroke='#222' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='100' cy='20' r='5' fill='#c33'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An oval running track with a starting point marked at the top.
questions.solution: Running in opposite directions, their combined speed is 5 + 3 = 8 m/s. They meet when combined distance = 400 m. Time = 400 / 8 = 50 seconds.

questions.question_text: How many positive integers less than 100 are divisible by 3 but not by 5?
questions.solution: Multiples of 3 less than 100: floor(99/3) = 33. Multiples of 15 (divisible by both 3 and 5) less than 100: 15, 30, 45, 60, 75, 90 = 6 values. Answer: 33 - 6 = 27.
